sanskrit:
यदक्षरं वेदविदो वदन्ति विशन्ति यद्यतयो वीतरागाः।
यदिच्छन्तो ब्रह्मचर्यं चरन्ति तत्ते पदं संग्रहेण प्रवक्ष्ये॥
transliteration:
yad akṣaraṃ veda-vido vadanti viśanti yad yatayo vīta-rāgāḥ |
yad icchanto brahmacaryaṃ caranti tat te padaṃ saṅgraheṇa pravakṣye ||
literal_meaning: That syllable OM which Vedic knowers proclaim, into which the passion-free sages enter, desiring which they practice celibacy—that state I shall briefly declare to you.
contextual_meaning: Krishna offers Arjuna a compressed map of the ‘impersonal’ path: single-pointed focus on the deathless sound OM, sustained by sexual restraint, leading to absorption in Brahman. It is a psychological technology for transcending fear of mortality through disciplined attention and energy conservation.
psychological_state: Pre-contemplative arousal—Arjuna is alert, wanting a concise protocol for ultimate safety amid existential threat.
trigger_scenario: A 30-something professional in therapy who compulsively scrolls hook-up apps to outrun mortality fears hears the verse and wonders if redirecting sexual energy into a daily 10-minute OM-chant could quiet the panic.
anchor_text: Chant OM with celibate focus to dissolve death-fear into timeless peace.
